executive_summary.one_liner: An AI‑native CLI that pairs a local Copilot‑style agent with on‑demand access to Claude Code, Gemini Code Assist, and other remote coding agents — all inside a single terminal session.
executive_summary.why_kraken: Developers need an AI coding partner that works offline for fast local tasks (copilot) and can escalate to cloud‑grade reasoning when the problem is hard — without switching tools, copying context, or leaving the terminal.
key_tenets: 1. AI agents must work together — local copilot + cloud agents in the same conversation.
2. Every model call counts — no hidden costs or opaque token usage.
3. Security is not optional — sandbox execution, policy enforcement, audit trails.
4. Parity with Claude Code is the floor — not the ceiling.

personas.name: Solo Developer (Alex)
personas.pain: Context‑switching between Copilot for quick completions and Claude Code for complex refactors. No unified billing or session history.
personas.want: One CLI that intelligently routes requests — fast local completions for simple edits, cloud escalation for architecture decisions.

personas.name: Team Lead (Jordan)
personas.pain: No visibility into what agents are used by the team. Security risks from unvetted cloud prompts. No cost control.
personas.want: Audit trails, per‑developer usage limits, sandbox execution, and a single policy file (kraken.toml) that enforces rules across the org.

personas.name: Security Engineer (Taylor)
personas.pain: Cannot allow LLM agents that exfiltrate code. Existing tools have no data‑loss prevention (DLP) or prompt‑injection scanning.
personas.want: Policy‑based prompt filters, sandboxed subprocess execution, network egress controls, and signed audit logs.

personas.name: Platform Maintainer
personas.pain: Wants to integrate Kraken into the internal dev portal but needs API‑driven triggers, event hooks, and a service mesh identity model.
personas.want: First‑class headless mode, JSON‑streaming output, webhook notification on long‑running tasks, and OpenTelemetry tracing.

functional_requirements.title: Multi‑agent orchestration
functional_requirements.description: Support multiple AI agent backends (local copilot, Claude Code, Gemini Code Assist, OpenAI agents) in a single session. Agent selection is automatic based on task complexity, but overridable.
acceptance: User can invoke any registered agent with /agent‑name
Automatic escalation from local to cloud when task exceeds local capability threshold
Session context is shared across agents (within privacy boundaries)
No context duplication — one history, one token budget

functional_requirements.title: First‑class offline mode
functional_requirements.description: Kraken copilot agent must work fully offline using local models (e.g., llama.cpp, ONNX, or similar). No phone‑home, no mandatory cloud dependency.
acceptance: Copilot completions and inline chat work with zero internet connectivity
Local model can be swapped via kraken.toml
Graceful degradation — cloud agent calls are queued or blocked when offline
Explicit user consent required before any network request

functional_requirements.title: Claude Code parity (baseline)
functional_requirements.description: Kraken must match or exceed Claude Code's feature set for daily coding workflows.
acceptance: Inline chat, edit, problem resolution, agentic loop, file operations
Git integration (commit messages, PR summaries, changelog generation)
Multi‑file diff display and chunked application
Permission system with allow/deny/ask
See PARITY.md for the full matrix

functional_requirements.title: Sandboxed execution
functional_requirements.description: All agent‑triggered commands run inside a sandbox with resource limits, network egress policy, and filesystem access controls.
acceptance: Commands are intercepted and policy‑checked before execution
Network egress can be blocked, allowed, or prompted per‑domain
CPU/memory/time limits per command
Full audit trail of every executed command and its output
Sandbox bypass requires explicit user approval and is logged

functional_requirements.title: Data‑loss prevention for prompts
functional_requirements.description: Prompts sent to cloud agents must be scanned for secrets, PII, and policy violations. User is warned or blocked before transmission.
acceptance: Regex‑based and entropy‑based secret detection on outgoing prompts
Custom allow/block lists in kraken.toml
Redaction mode — automatically replaces secrets before sending
Audit log of all blocked or redacted prompts

functional_requirements.title: Policy‑driven configuration
functional_requirements.description: All security, cost, and agent behaviour is controlled via a single kraken.toml file. Team‑wide policy can be enforced via signed or git‑tracked config.
acceptance: Policy file supports agent allowlist, cost caps, sandbox rules, DLP rules, audit settings
Team‑wide override (e.g., /etc/kraken/policy.toml) that users cannot bypass
Policy changes are logged and can trigger notifications

functional_requirements.title: Cost visibility and control
functional_requirements.description: Every cloud API call is tracked, billed to a project/task, and visible in real time. Users set hard and soft spend limits.
acceptance: Per‑session, per‑agent, and cumulative cost displayed in status bar
Hard spend limits stop cloud calls when exceeded
Soft spend limits warn the user
Cost attribution via environment variable or flag (--project, --task)

functional_requirements.title: Immutable audit trail
functional_requirements.description: All agent interactions, commands, policy decisions, and cost events are recorded in a tamper‑evident audit log.
acceptance: Audit log is append‑only and optionally signed
Logs include timestamp, agent id, prompt hash, command, policy decision, cost
Audit export in JSON, CSV, and OpenTelemetry formats

functional_requirements.title: Plugin system
functional_requirements.description: Third‑party agents and tools can be added via a plugin interface without modifying Kraken core.
acceptance: Plugin manifest (kraken‑plugin.toml) defines agent name, binary path, schema, cost model
Plugins run in their own sandbox
Plugin marketplace or registry is out of scope for v0.x

functional_requirements.title: Headless / CI mode
functional_requirements.description: Kraken runs in non‑interactive mode for CI/CD pipelines, pre‑commit hooks, and batch processing.
acceptance: All output is JSON‑streamed or structured
Non‑zero exit code on failures
Can read input from stdin / file and write output to stdout / file
No TUI dependency in headless mode

functional_requirements.title: Event hooks and webhooks
functional_requirements.description: External systems can react to Kraken events (prompt sent, command executed, policy violation, cost threshold hit) via webhooks or Unix sockets.
acceptance: Configurable webhook URLs per event type
Webhook payload includes full event context
Unix socket delivery option for local services

functional_requirements.title: OpenTelemetry instrumentation
functional_requirements.description: Kraken exports traces, metrics, and logs via OpenTelemetry for observability in production deployments.
acceptance: Traces for agent calls, sandbox execution, policy checks
Metrics for token usage, latency, cost, error rates
Optional export to stdout, file, or OTLP endpoint

non_functional_requirements.title: Sub‑100ms copilot latency
non_functional_requirements.description: Local copilot completions must appear in under 100ms on modern hardware (M1 Mac, 12th‑gen Intel). Cloud agent startup overhead must be under 2s.
non_functional_requirements.measure: p95 latency measured by built‑in benchmark suite

non_functional_requirements.title: Memory ceiling
non_functional_requirements.description: Kraken idle memory must stay under 50MB. Peak memory during local copilot inference under 2GB.

non_functional_requirements.title: Sandbox escape resistance
non_functional_requirements.description: The sandbox must resist fork bombs, resource exhaustion, and filesystem escape attempts by an untrusted agent.
non_functional_requirements.measure: Red‑team test suite with known escape vectors. Zero critical escapes.

non_functional_requirements.title: Platform coverage
non_functional_requirements.description: Kraken must ship as a single static binary for Linux (x64, aarch64, armv7), macOS (x64, aarch64), Windows (x64), and FreeBSD (x64).
non_functional_requirements.measure: Release pipeline produces 7 signed artifacts per version

open_questions: Do we need a hosted relay for enterprise audit aggregation? → defer to 0.5
Should Kraken support MCP (Model Context Protocol) natively? → yes, planned for 0.4
Plugin sandbox: Wasm or containers? → investigate Wasm for v0.4
Cost model: per‑token billing or per‑request billing? → align with Claude Code billing model
future_scope: Multi‑player session sharing (pair debugging with remote peer)
Kraken Cloud — hosted relay with team dashboards, audit aggregation, policy distribution
Plugin marketplace with signing and verification
VSCode / JetBrains extension that embeds the Kraken TUI
Kraken as a GitHub App (auto‑fix PRs, review comments)

glossary.agent: A backend AI provider (Copilot, Claude Code, Gemini Code Assist, OpenAI)
glossary.krakenhip: Internal service that routes and tracks agent calls within a session; named by analogy to a squid's feeding arm
glossary.session: A single Kraken invocation from start to exit, with full conversation history
glossary.sandbox: An isolated execution environment with resource limits and egress controls
glossary.dwell time: The time a user spends inside an agent before switching agents or exiting
glossary.dogfood: Workflow scope label used by the Kraken team for internal self‑hosting; currently kraken-dogfood in production
glossary.pinpoint: A follow‑up task or question spawned from an ongoing agent conversation
